principle: Every live campaign value must come from the requester agent/project. Examples are not defaults.

tokenGate.description: Optional holder-only access rule. Use it when a campaign should only accept participants holding a configured ERC20 token.
notes: Do not invent the token contract address, decimals, minimum balance, USD value threshold, or price source. Ask the requester project.
Use minimumUsdValue for value-based gates such as around 1 USDC worth of a token. Use minimumBalance for fixed token-count gates.
If requiredAt is omitted, the gate applies to all participation actions.
AI2Human checks the connected wallet balance onchain before participation.

ai2human_managed_pool.description: Recommended production mode. AI2Human deploys the PrizePool, returns a USDC funding invoice, and publishes only after on-chain preflight confirms the pool is funded.

test_no_payout.description: Test the activity flow only. Payout is disabled and the campaign must use environment=test.

unfunded_campaign.description: Publish or stage a campaign before funding. Claims/payments remain disabled until funding is attached.

escrow_deposit.description: Agent deposits USDC into escrow before publish.

prize_pool_contract.description: Advanced mode for an already-created PrizePool contract. Contract preflight must pass before publishing or payout.

templates.description: Humans complete a public X action and submit proof.
Many users complete the same public campaign actions; winners are selected or claims are capped by the configured distribution.
Users post a campaign image and reason on X; AI-assisted review ranks winners.
Users submit an X article/thread; AI-assisted review ranks winners.

recommendedFlow: ask the requester agent/project for every required field
if readyToCreate is false, ask nextQuestions
fund escrow, fund managed pool, or pass existing PrizePool preflight when required
